ops.title: Fix ccx diff in jj-colocated repos — commit-range resolution approach
ops.body:
# Fix `ccx diff` in jj-colocated repos (restore structural symbols)

## Context

`ccx diff` is degenerate in jj-colocated repos: every working-copy source
(`uncommitted`/`HEAD`/`@-`) lists the changed files but prints `(0 symbols)` and
**zero hunks** per file. Reported against installed `ccx 0.2.0`.

I verified the real mechanism by running the vendored tilth binary directly
(`~/Library/Caches/cc-context/bin/tilth-v0.9.0`) against this repo — **not** by
inference:

| Command | Result |
|---|---|
| `tilth diff` (uncommitted) | 18 files, **0 symbols** each |
| `tilth diff HEAD` | 18 files, **0 symbols** each |
| `tilth diff HEAD~1` | 21 files, **0 symbols** each |
| `tilth diff HEAD~3..HEAD~2` (committed range) | **works** — "2 modified, 6 added", `[~] build_run_spec`, `[+] drive` |
| `tilth diff <@-sha>..<@sha>` (resolved commit range) | **works** — "10 modified, 38 added", full per-symbol output |

**Root cause:** tilth's structural diff is correct on commit-to-commit ranges, but
returns 0 symbols whenever the **working tree** is one side. In a colocated jj repo
jj keeps the git index equal to `HEAD`/`@-` (it never stages working changes), so
tilth's working-side read sees index == HEAD → before == after → 0 symbols. Passing
tilth "the right ref" does *not* help (`tilth diff HEAD` still fails) — the
working-tree *read* is what's blind.

**The fix (the user's Option 2, corrected):** in jj the working copy `@` is itself a
real git commit object. So "uncommitted" ≡ `@-..@` ≡ a commit-to-commit range tilth
diffs perfectly. Resolve `@-` and `@` to git SHAs and feed tilth that range →
structural symbols restored. The existing `SupplementDiff` textual fallback stays for
non-code files (json/toml/txt), which tilth legitimately reports as 0 symbols.

The working copy already contains an in-flight diff refactor (`internal/render/diff.go`
`SupplementDiff`/`RunDiffCLI`, modified `internal/vcs/vcs.go`) wired into both the CLI
(`internal/cli/run.go:37`) and the MCP proxy (`internal/proxy/proxy.go:59`). 0.2.0
predates it. This plan **extends** that refactor to produce structural symbols rather
than only textual hunks.

**Scope (per the user):** restore structural symbols in jj (Option 2); also fix the
latent `staged` bug and harden the supplement regex; then **ship v0.3.0**.

## Approach

### 1. jj working-copy diff → commit range (`internal/vcs/vcs.go`) — the core

The working-tree side of any jj diff must become `@`'s resolved commit SHA.

- Add an injectable working-copy resolver (mirror the existing `branchLookup`
  pattern at `vcs.go:95-97`/`defaultBranch` at `vcs.go:172`) that shells
  `jj log --no-graph -r <rev> -T commit_id` for `@` and `@-`. The `@` call triggers
  jj's snapshot, so the SHA reflects the live working copy. (`@-` reliably equals git
  `HEAD` in colocated repos — confirmed `HEAD == @- == 50388138` here — so it may be
  resolved via `HEAD`; resolving both via jj is fine too.) Fail loud if `jj` errors
  (we are in a jj repo; `%w`-wrap, no silent fallback — STYLEGUIDE §Errors).
- In `resolveJJ` (`vcs.go:99`), change the `translationWorkingTree` case
  (`vcs.go:101-102`) from returning `translated=""` (bare `tilth diff`) to returning
  `translated = "<@-sha>..<@sha>"`, `useTilth=true`. `translateRevset` stays a pure
  classifier (still returns `translationWorkingTree`); the I/O resolution lives in
  `resolveJJ`, which already does I/O for the default branch.
- Generalize the same principle to the other working-vs-base sources the user listed
  as broken (they share the root cause): a working-vs-`<ref>` jj source becomes
  `<ref>..<@sha>` (e.g. `HEAD` → `HEAD..<@sha>`; `@-` stays an alias for uncommitted
  → `<@-sha>..<@sha>`, matching today's behavior). tilth resolves the git-ref left
  side; only jj revsets (`@-`) need pre-resolution to a SHA. Genuinely jj-only
  revsets (`::`, `@`, `|`…) keep the existing `jj diff` fallback (`jjFallbackArgv`,
  `vcs.go:161`) untouched.

### 2. Thread the resolved range; supplement reuses it (`vcs.go`, `render/diff.go`, `tilth.go`)

`RawHunkArgv` (`vcs.go:77`) currently re-calls `ResolveDiffSource` — which would
re-snapshot jj **per supplemented file**. Resolve the jj range **once per diff** and
reuse it for both the tilth invocation and every raw-hunk fetch:
- `RunDiffCLI` (`render/diff.go:27`) resolves the source once; its `fetch` closure
  builds `git -C <dir> diff <range> -- <file>` from the pre-resolved range instead of
  re-resolving. Both the structural pass and the textual supplement then use an
  identical base.

### 3. Supplement regex must match commit-range headers (`render/diff.go:19`)

Commit-range output has **no `c/`…`w/` prefix** — verified: `## bench/ccxbench/__main__.py (9 symbols)`,
`## .claude/hooks/packs.toml (0 symbols)`. The current regex
`^## c/\S+ w/(\S+) \((\d+) symbols\)$` will not match these, so the textual fallback
for 0-symbol non-code files silently breaks. Broaden it to accept both the optional
working-tree prefix (`c/`…`w/`, and the index `i/` variant) **and** the bare
commit-range path:

```go
^## (?:[a-z]/\S+ w/)?(\S+) \((\d+) symbols\)$
```

The trailing `w/`, ` (`, ` symbols)`, `$` anchors stay strict.

### 4. Fix the `staged` latent bug (`vcs.go`)

tilth natively accepts `staged` (its `--help` lists `uncommitted | staged | <ref>`;
`tilth diff staged` → "No changes" correctly), so the **structural** side already
works via passthrough. The only break is the raw-hunk fetch: `RawHunkArgv` would emit
`git diff staged -- <file>` (fatal: ambiguous ref). Add a `staged` branch so the
hunk fetch is `git -C <dir> diff --cached -- <file>`. Add an explicit `case "staged"`
to `translateRevset` (returning a `translationStaged` class) for clarity and
table-testing. In jj there is no index, so `staged` is correctly empty — no error
needed.

### 5. Tests

- **`internal/vcs/vcs_test.go`** — extend `TestTranslateRevset`, `TestResolveJJ`
  (inject a fake working-copy resolver returning fixed SHAs, assert the `A..B` range),
  and `TestRawHunkArgv` (jj uncommitted → `git diff <range> -- file`; `staged` →
  `git diff --cached -- file`; **keep the git-repo cases unchanged as the regression
  guard** that the jj path never leaks into plain git).
- **`internal/render/diff_test.go`** — add a real-jj integration test mirroring
  `TestRunDiffCLISupplementsRawHunk`: an `initJJRepo(t)` helper (`jj git init
  --colocate`, `jj config set --repo user.{name,email}`, seed + `jj commit`, then dirty
  the file) and assert `RunDiffCLI` yields **structural symbols** for a code file and
  a **textual hunk** for a non-code file. Guard with `exec.LookPath("jj")` →
  `t.Skip` (CI installs no jj). Add supplement unit cases for the commit-range
  (`## a.go (0 symbols)`) and `i/`-prefix header formats.

### 6. Docs + release

- Update the jj-aware claim sites to reflect the commit-range mechanism:
  `AGENTS.md:78`, `README.md:91` (and the `bench/ccxbench/ladder.txt:17` mirror).
- **Ship v0.3.0** (user-approved): land the fix on `main`, then
  `git tag v0.3.0 origin/main` → `release.yml`/goreleaser → Homebrew formula publish.
  Optionally add a `jj` install step to `.github/workflows/ci.yml` so the integration
  test runs in CI instead of skipping (recommended, since we're shipping a jj fix).

## Potential Pitfalls

- **Commit-range headers have no `c/`/`w/` prefix** — the regex change (step 3) is
  load-bearing; without it the structural fix works but non-code files lose their
  textual fallback. Easy to miss because code files (which now have symbols) look fine.
- **Don't pass tilth a ref and expect symbols** — `tilth diff HEAD` is still blind.
  Only a commit *range* (`@-..@`) works. The fix is the range, not the ref.
- **jj snapshot per file** — naive `RawHunkArgv` re-resolution re-snapshots jj for
  every supplemented file. Resolve once and thread (step 2), or you pay N snapshots
  and risk inconsistent `@` SHAs mid-diff.
- **Keep the git path untouched** — plain-git `uncommitted` legitimately means
  working-vs-index; the commit-range/`@`-SHA logic must be gated on `Detect == JJ`.
  The unchanged git-repo test cases are the regression guard.
- **`jj config set --repo`** in the test (not global) — never mutate the developer's
  real jj config.
- **CI has no jj** — the integration test must `t.Skip` on missing `jj`, or every CI
  run goes red. (Or add the jj install step.)
- **Working copy holds unrelated in-flight work** (grok, bench changes). Land the diff
  fix as its own atomic commit (the `vcs.go`/`render/diff.go`/`tilth.go`/tests group);
  coordinate separately what else rides into v0.3.0.
- **The `@` SHA is live** — resolving `@` snapshots and can mint a new commit object
  each time the working copy changes; that's correct (matches `jj diff`), just don't
  cache it across edits.

## Workflow Plan

The main agent tracks state, dispatches, reviews each phase result, and runs the
release. Substantive work runs as a `Workflow`:

| Phase | Shape | Agents | Verification |
|---|---|---|---|
| Implement | single (coherent, interdependent edits in one worktree) | 1 implementer — steps 1–5 across `vcs.go`/`render/diff.go`/`tilth.go`/`run.go`/`proxy.go`/tests | `task build` + `task test` green inside the worktree |
| Verify | parallel | (a) build + run `ccx diff` / `ccx diff staged` in this jj repo, diff against `tilth diff <@-sha>..<@sha>`; (b) full `go test -race ./...`; (c) adversarial review for git-repo regression + per-file snapshot cost | structural symbols present in jj output; git path byte-identical to before; no extra jj snapshots |
| Release | single | main agent — `git tag v0.3.0 origin/main`, watch `release.yml` + goreleaser + Homebrew | `gh run watch` green; `brew upgrade ccx` → 0.3.0; `ccx diff` shows symbols |

The Implement phase is one agent because the edits are tightly coupled (the
translation, threading, and regex changes interlock); fanning out would conflict. The
Verify phase fans out because build-run, test, and review are independent.

## Verification

End-to-end, in this jj-colocated repo:

1. `task build` (or `go build -o ./bin/ccx ./cmd/ccx`).
2. `./bin/ccx diff` → expect **structural symbols** (~"10 modified, 38 added", e.g.
   `[~] verify_oss`, `[+] go_funcs`) for code files **and** textual hunks for non-code
   files (`.claude/hooks/packs.toml`, `*.json`). Cross-check against
   `tilth diff "$(jj log --no-graph -r @- -T commit_id)..$(jj log --no-graph -r @ -T commit_id)"`.
3. `./bin/ccx diff staged` → no `fatal: ambiguous argument`; correct (empty here).
   In a scratch **git** repo with a staged change, `ccx diff staged` shows the staged
   structural diff + `git diff --cached` hunk.
4. `task test` (`go test -race ./...`) green; the new jj integration test runs locally
   (jj present) and skips where jj is absent.
5. Pre-commit hooks (gofumpt/goimports/golangci-lint) pass on commit.
6. After tagging: `gh run watch "$(gh run list -L1 --json databaseId -q '.[0].databaseId')" --exit-status`
   green; `brew upgrade ccx` installs 0.3.0; `ccx diff` in this repo shows symbols.
ops.when: before implementing or modifying the ccx diff structural-symbols behavior for jj-colocated repos
